mechanism_narrative: When real activity in consumer-facing demand is rising, measured by positive 3-month momentum in retail sales, firms receive stronger revenue signals and S&P 500 earnings expectations are revised upward, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS value is 384631.0 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if months with positive 3-month RSAFS momentum do not deliver higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than months with negative momentum, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals deteriorating household income and recession risk, causing equity investors to lower forward cash-flow expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE value is 5.7 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is accelerating while the policy rate is already above its 12-month average, discount-rate pressure dominates nominal revenue growth and lowers equity valuation multiples, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates for 3 consecutive months and FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:latest available CPIAUCSL value is 219.181 and FEDFUNDS value is 2.01
failure_condition: Reject if this joint inflation-policy trigger has nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if adding the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample directional accuracy versus separate CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS signals.

mechanism_narrative: Positive short-term momentum in industrial production indicates improving operating leverage and stronger goods-sector demand, leading investors to raise cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO value is 111.8419 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum is not associated with higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative momentum in a walk-forward test, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in housing starts signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future pressure on construction, durable-goods, and credit channels, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average after being above it in the prior month
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST value is 1066.0 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if downside HOUST moving-average crossings are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment is expanding and unemployment is falling simultaneously, labor income growth supports consumption without confirming a jobless recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE falls over the same 3-month window
evidence_refs: fact:latest available PAYEMS value is 137615.0 and UNRATE value is 5.7
regime_hint: early-to-mid expansion with improving labor absorption
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down signal does not outperform either single-series signal in out-of-sample 20-day return prediction or if its triggered hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A meaningful decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing and lower discount rates, causing rapid valuation relief in equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS value is 2.01 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day S&P 500 returns after FEDFUNDS declines of at least 0.25 percentage points are not higher than unconditional 5-day returns or if the triggered directional hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth is slowing while payroll growth is also decelerating, broad demand and labor income weaken together, leading investors to cut earnings forecasts and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth declines versus the prior quarter and PAYEMS 3-month growth decelerates versus the prior 3-month window
evidence_refs: fact:latest available GDPC1 value is 11700.6 and PAYEMS value is 137615.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 slowdown and PAYEMS deceleration signal is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or if interaction coefficients are not negative in walk-forward estimation.
